title: HyperAgent Manifest
description: Shared interface contract for agents that can run in both Hyper-Vibe Course (.agents/) and HyperCode V2.4 (Hyper-Agents-Box)

name.description: kebab-case, 3–50 chars, globally unique within a deployment

version.description: semver — increment minor for new tools, patch for fixes

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in Discord and Mission Control UI

author.description: Discord username or GitHub handle of the builder

runtime.description: Determines which base Docker image is used at graduation

entrypoint.description: Relative path from manifest.json to the main file (e.g. main.py, index.ts)

tools.description: MCP-compatible tool definitions — must have at least 1

description.description: What the tool does — shown to the LLM calling it

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters

output_schema.description: Optional JSON Schema describing tool output shape

mcp_compatible.description: Set true only if the agent implements the MCP SSE transport. Required for V2.4 gateway registration.

memory.description: Backing store for agent state. 'none' = stateless. V2.4 provides redis + postgres automatically when docker compose runs.

env_vars.description: List of required environment variable names (values NOT stored here). The graduate script will prompt for missing ones.

port.description: ⚠️ REQUIRED if mcp_compatible=true. Port in 3100–3999 range that the agent's MCP server listens on. Must not clash with other agents in the same deployment. V2.4 MCP gateway reverse-proxies this.

health_check.description: HTTP path for container health check (e.g. /health). Defaults to /health if absent.

course_level.description: Minimum Course progression level required to build/use this agent template. 1=HyperNewbie, 5=BROski Elite
